name: node-mimic-response
title: node-mimic-response
summary: Mimic a Node.js HTTP response stream
description:
Mimic a Node.js HTTP response stream
Make a function mimic another one.
.
Useful when you wrap a function and like to preserve the original name and
other properties.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-mimic-response because Mimic a Node.js HTTP response stream.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-mimic-response as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-mimic-response with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-mimic-response --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
